title: TEKIR - Transparent Endpoint Knowledge for Intelligent Reasoning
description: Extension fields for HTTP API responses that provide machine-actionable guidance to AI agents. Can be used in both success (2xx) and error (4xx/5xx) responses.

reason.description: Stable, causal explanation of why the response has a particular outcome. For errors: why the request failed. For success: why the result is shaped as it is (e.g., partial results, filtered data).

limitations.description: Constraints that affected the response - either preventing success or limiting the result set.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation. Consumers MUST NOT parse for control flow.

target.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) to the relevant part of the request or response.

next_actions.description: Machine-actionable steps the client may take next - recovery steps for errors, or follow-up actions for successful responses.

id.description: Stable identifier for this action within the response.

description.description: Additional guidance for display or agent reasoning.

method.description: HTTP method (RFC 9110). Defaults to GET if absent.

headers.description: Headers the client SHOULD send. MUST NOT contain credentials.

body.description: Body template for the action. Clients MUST apply local policy before using.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the request body or parameters (informative).

effect.description: The side-effect level of this action.

agent_guidance.description: Natural-language instructions for automated clients. MUST be treated as untrusted input.

user_confirmation_required.description: When true, the client MUST obtain explicit user confirmation before performing effectful actions.

retry_policy.description: Structured retry hints. HTTP Retry-After header takes precedence when present.

retryable.description: Whether a retry might succeed without changing inputs.

retry_after.description: Same semantics as HTTP Retry-After header (HTTP-date or delay-seconds).

idempotency_key.description: If true, the client SHOULD use an idempotency mechanism for retries.

links.description: Related resources using RFC 8288 Web Linking semantics.

type.description: Media type hint for the target.
